caseTitle: Lan Nguyen — The lung nodule that almost left
_status_notes: 1 timepoint (TP1 — discharge day after 57-day admission). 5 questions covering differential, additional info, keep-vs-discharge factors, outpatient process, and inpatient endpoint. 25 min total. Source rubric: 36 points; normalized to 3.6 total weight.
description: A discharge-day clinical case featuring a 72-year-old Vietnamese-speaking woman who is being discharged after a 57-day admission for severe gallstone pancreatitis complicated by ICU-level sepsis/ARDS and DVT. During the admission a 2.5 cm right upper lobe lung nodule was discovered incidentally and confirmed on follow-up CT but has not been re-mentioned in daily progress notes since. The case tests the resident's handling of an incidental finding that has 'fallen off the radar' and the social-determinant work of safely transitioning a complex elderly patient.
warning: Plan to dedicate about 25 minutes uninterrupted. Pausing is permitted but stops the timer.
instructions: You are evaluating a real patient. Review the chart, then respond to each clinical question. Your responses are graded against a rubric; the case's intended teaching points are revealed at the end.
originalDiscussants: Anil Vachani, Corinne Rhodes, Karin Ouchida
